sections.title: Portfolio Overview
sections.prose: Aajil is an SME lending fintech providing ultra-short-term raw materials trade credit to industrial SMEs in Saudi Arabia. The fund purchases raw materials from pre-vetted suppliers and instantly sells on credit to manufacturers, contractors, and wholesale traders. Revenue is locked at origination (margin + admin fee). Asset-light structure — Aajil never holds inventory.

metrics.name: AUM (Outstanding)
metrics.formula: Sum of outstanding balances across all active transactions

metrics.name: GMV Disbursed
metrics.formula: Cumulative value of all disbursements to date

metrics.name: Total Transactions
metrics.formula: Count of all credit transactions originated

metrics.name: Active Customers
metrics.formula: Count of distinct customers with active facilities

metrics.name: Avg Deals per Customer
metrics.formula: Total transactions / Total customers served

metrics.name: Credit-to-Revenue Ratio
metrics.formula: Credit facility / Customer annual revenue

notes: Current data from investor deck (Mar 2026). Loan tape from Cascade Debt pending.
AUM (SAR 120M) is outstanding balance. GMV (>SAR 340M) is cumulative disbursements.
Currency: SAR (Saudi Riyal). USD conversion at 0.2667.

sections.title: Traction (Volume & Balance)
sections.prose: Traction measures origination volume (new credit deployed per period) and outstanding balance (AUM). Inspired by the Cascade Debt reporting format, the platform shows both Volume and Balance views with MoM/QoQ/YoY growth rates for context.

metrics.name: Monthly Disbursement Volume
metrics.formula: Sum of new disbursements in the period

metrics.name: Outstanding Balance
metrics.formula: Sum of principal outstanding across all active transactions

metrics.name: MoM Growth
metrics.formula: (Current month volume - Prior month) / Prior month

metrics.name: QoQ Growth
metrics.formula: (Current quarter volume - Prior quarter) / Prior quarter

metrics.name: YoY Growth
metrics.formula: (Current year volume - Prior year) / Prior year

notes: With tape data: monthly granularity. Current data: annual granularity from investor deck.

sections.title: Cash Conversion & Collections
sections.prose: For instalment-based SME credit, cash conversion is measured through collection rates by vintage and instalment on-time rates. The Cascade Debt platform tracks 'Cash Collected by Cohort' — the percentage of each monthly vintage's disbursement that has been repaid.

metrics.name: Collection Rate (GLR)
metrics.formula: Total collected / Total disbursed per vintage

metrics.name: Instalment On-Time Rate
metrics.formula: Instalments paid on or before due date / Total instalments due

metrics.name: DSO
metrics.formula: Cash-weighted days from disbursement to collection

metrics.name: Principal Collection Rate
metrics.formula: Principal collected / Principal disbursed per vintage

notes: Total collections include principal + margin + admin fee. Principal-only view isolates capital recovery.
Cascade offers 'Payment Breakdown' toggle to split total vs principal collections.

sections.title: Credit Quality (Delinquency & DPD)
sections.prose: Aajil uses contractual DPD (Days Past Due) as the primary credit quality clock. The platform tracks 4 DPD thresholds — 7, 30, 60, and 90 days — with DPD 7 serving as an early warning signal unique to SME trade credit (shorter tenor means earlier signals matter). DPD 60+ triggers the Legal collections phase.

metrics.name: PAR 7+
metrics.formula: Outstanding with DPD > 7 / Total active outstanding

metrics.name: PAR 30+
metrics.formula: Outstanding with DPD > 30 / Total active outstanding

metrics.name: PAR 60+
metrics.formula: Outstanding with DPD > 60 / Total active outstanding

metrics.name: PAR 90+
metrics.formula: Outstanding with DPD > 90 / Total active outstanding

metrics.name: Rolling Default Rate
metrics.formula: DPD>X balance / Total originated (rolling window)

notes: DPD 7 is Aajil-specific: early warning for short-tenor credit (4-5 month avg). Configurable via dpd_thresholds in config.
Rolling Default Rate follows Cascade Debt methodology: tracks cumulative default rate over time.
Self-reported DPD60+ < 5% (trailing 12 months as of Dec 2025).

sections.title: Trust Score System
sections.prose: Aajil's proprietary Trust Score system (1-5, Green to Critical) drives collections strategy. Each borrower receives a score based on payment history, which determines the collections approach: Green (5) receives enablement-first support, while Critical (1-2) triggers 48-hour payment deadlines and legal preparation.

metrics.name: Trust Score Distribution
metrics.formula: Count of active customers by trust score (1-5)

metrics.name: Score Migration
metrics.formula: Trust score changes by customer over time

notes: Green (5): Good history. Amber (4): Minor delays. Red (3): Repeated issues. Critical (1-2): Severe risk.
Collections phases: Preventive (-15 to 0 DPD), Active (1-60 DPD), Legal (60+ DPD).
SIMAH: NPL notice at 150 days, auto-reporting at 180 days.

sections.title: Vintage / Cohort Analysis
sections.prose: Vintage analysis tracks delinquency development by Month on Books (MoB) for each origination cohort. The Cascade Debt platform provides vintage curves with configurable DPD thresholds (30/60/90), measurement basis (Original Balance vs Number of Loans), cohort type (Loan vs Borrower), and a cure toggle (With/Without Cure).

metrics.name: Vintage DPD Curve
metrics.formula: DPD>X rate at each MoB for each origination vintage

metrics.name: Cure Rate
metrics.formula: Loans cured (returned to current) / Loans that were delinquent

notes: Cascade supports Loan-level and Borrower-level cohorts (dedup by customer's first transaction).
'Without Cure' mode counts a loan as defaulted once even if it later cures — shows true loss exposure.
Color-coded heatmap table: green (low DPD) to red (high DPD).

sections.title: Loss Attribution
sections.prose: Loss attribution tracks the waterfall from origination through default to recovery. Default is defined as DPD 60+ (the Legal phase trigger). Recovery includes promissory note enforcement and asset recovery. Net loss = Gross default - Recoveries.

metrics.name: Gross Default Rate
metrics.formula: DPD60+ balance / Originated per vintage

metrics.name: Recovery Rate
metrics.formula: Recovered post-default / Gross default balance

metrics.name: Net Loss Rate
metrics.formula: (Default - Recovery) / Originated per vintage

metrics.name: LGD
metrics.formula: Net loss / Gross default per defaulted transaction

metrics.name: Yield
metrics.formula: (Margin + Admin fee) / Disbursed amount

notes: Promissory notes provide additional recovery channel beyond standard collections.
Asset-based lending (raw materials) provides some recovery floor vs unsecured credit.

sections.title: Concentration
sections.prose: Concentration risk is tracked across customer type (Manufacturer/Contractor/Trader), individual customer exposure, supplier concentration, and sector/industry diversification.

metrics.name: HHI (Customer)
metrics.formula: Sum of squared customer shares

metrics.name: Top-N Customer Share
metrics.formula: Top 5/10 customers as % of AUM

metrics.name: Customer Type Mix
metrics.formula: AUM by Manufacturer / Contractor / Trader

notes: Segmentation mirrors Cascade 'Display by' dimensions: Customer Type, Product Type, Term Group.

sections.title: Underwriting
sections.prose: Aajil uses a 4-stage underwriting process: Sales Screening, Risk Assessment, Credit Report, and Credit Decision Committee (CDC). Financial ratio thresholds vary by customer type. Non-CDC approval is permitted for facilities under SAR 400K that meet all assessment criteria.

metrics.name: Avg Assessment Time
metrics.formula: Hours from complete documentation to decision

metrics.name: Approval Rate
metrics.formula: Approved / Total applications

metrics.name: CDC Escalation Rate
metrics.formula: Applications requiring CDC review / Total

notes: Min revenue: SAR 5M. Max leverage: 50% with current ratio < 1.0. Max funded exposure: 30% of revenue.
Financial thresholds differ by customer type — documented in Financial Ratio Thresholds section.

sections.title: Forward-Looking Signals
sections.prose: Forward signals combine trust score migration (leading DPD indicator), underwriting drift (vintage-over-vintage quality changes), concentration trends (HHI time series), and growth rates (MoM/QoQ/YoY).

metrics.name: Trust Score Migration
metrics.formula: Score changes per customer over consecutive periods

metrics.name: Underwriting Drift
metrics.formula: Per-vintage quality metrics vs historical norms

metrics.name: MoM/QoQ/YoY Growth
metrics.formula: Period-over-period growth in origination volume

notes: Trust score deterioration precedes DPD events — primary leading indicator for Aajil.

sections.title: Data Source & Caveats
sections.prose: Current analysis is based on the Aajil investor deck (Mar 2026, 47 pages). Loan-level tape data is available via Cascade Debt (app.cascadedebt.com) and will be ingested when provided. The tape will unlock full live computation across all metrics.
notes: All current metrics are static extractions from the investor deck — not live computations.
Cascade Debt provides: Traction, Delinquency (7/30/60/90 DPD), Collection, Cohort (vintage analysis).

Expected tape columns: Transaction_ID, Customer_ID, Customer_Type, Disbursement_Date, Maturity_Date, Disbursed_Amount, Outstanding_Amount, Total_Collected, DPD, Trust_Score, Loan_Status, Loan_Grade, Product_Type.
When tape arrives, this module transitions from JSON-parser to SILQ-style DataFrame compute functions.
